content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and a prime number PPP. Vertex 111 is the root of the tree and for each vvv (2‚Č§v‚Č§N2 \le v \le N2‚Č§v‚Č§N), the parent of vertex vvv is pvp_vpv‚Äč (pv<vp_v \lt vpv‚Äč<v).

A vertex sss is an ancestor of another vertex fff if sss lies on the path between 111 and fff (in particular, fff is its own ancestor). For two vertices uuu and vvv, let's define LCA(u,v)\mathrm{LCA}(u, v)LCA(u,v) as the vertex sss with the largest number which is an ancestor of both uuu and vvv. Similarly, for two vertices vvv and sss, let Lv,s\mathbb{L}_{v,s}Lv,s‚Äč be the set of all vertices uuu such that s=LCA(u,v)s = \mathrm{LCA}(u, v)s=LCA(u,v).

Let's define a set AP\mathbb{A}_PAP‚Äč containing all sequences (indexed from 111) with length NNN whose elements are integers between 000 and P‚ąí1P-1P‚ąí1 inclusive. For two sequences a,b‚ąąAPa, b \in \mathbb{A}_Pa,b‚ąąAP‚Äč, let's define an operation LCA-convolution as a sequence c=a‚ąóbc = a * bc=a‚ąób such that for each valid xxx, cx=(‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj‚ąąLi,xaibj)‚Äämod‚ÄäP‚ÄČ.c_x = \left(\sum\limits_{i=1}^N \sum\limits_{j \in \mathbb{L}_{i,x}} a_i b_j\right) \bmod P \,.cx‚Äč=(i=1‚ąĎN‚Äčj‚ąąLi,x‚Äč‚ąĎ‚Äčai‚Äčbj‚Äč)modP.

You are also given some sequence c‚ąąAPc \in \mathbb{A}_Pc‚ąąAP‚Äč. You have two tasks:


Find the number of sequences a‚ąąAPa \in \mathbb{A}_Pa‚ąąAP‚Äč such that c=a‚ąóac = a * ac=a‚ąóa. Since this number can be large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
Find a sequence a‚ąąAPa \in \mathbb{A}_Pa‚ąąAP‚Äč such that c=a‚ąóac = a * ac=a‚ąóa (if it exists).
constraints: 1‚Č§pi<i1 \le p_i \lt i1‚Č§pi‚Äč<i for each valid iii
0‚Č§ci<P0 \le c_i \lt P0‚Č§ci‚Äč<P for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and PPP.
The second line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers p2,p3,‚Ä¶,pNp_2, p_3, \ldots, p_Np2‚Äč,p3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äč,c2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines:


The first of these lines should contain a single integer ‚Äē the number of sequences from the first task modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
If there is no sequence a‚ąąAPa \in \mathbb{A}_Pa‚ąąAP‚Äč such that c=a‚ąóac = a * ac=a‚ąóa, the second line should contain the integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, the second line should contain NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč, where a‚ąąAPa \in \mathbb{A}_Pa‚ąąAP‚Äč and c=a‚ąóac = a * ac=a‚ąóa should hold. If there is more than one such sequence, you may find any one of them.
